title: Lottomatica Group SpA (LOTMY) Full Year 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Profit Growth and ...
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

EBITDA Growth: 21% year-on-year increase. Adjusted Net Profit Growth: 45% increase. Capital Returned to Shareholders: EUR375 million, or EUR1.6 per share, through dividends and buybacks. Net Leverage: 2.4x, in line with 2024. Synergies Implemented: EUR90 million, 34% more than initially announced. Market Share Gain: 1.2 points increase, reaching 31.3% in Q4 2024. Cost of Debt: 5.3%, 240 bps lower than IPO. Revenue Growth: 12% on a reported basis. EBITDA Margin: 38%, up from 35% in 2024. Online Revenue Growth: 22% increase. Sports Franchise Revenue Growth: 14% increase. Gaming Franchise Revenue Growth: 1% increase. Operating Cash Flow Growth: 18% increase, 24% excluding upfront concession CapEx. Net Financial Debt: EUR2.1 billion at year-end. Guidance for 2026 Revenue: EUR2.390 billion to EUR2.460 billion. Guidance for 2026 EBITDA: EUR940 million to EUR980 million. Dividend Proposal: EUR0.44 per share, totaling EUR111 million.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 7 Warning Sign with LOTMY. Is LOTMY fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Release Date: March 03, 2026

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript.

Positive Points

Lottomatica Group SpA (LOTMY) reported a 21% year-on-year growth in EBITDA and a 45% increase in adjusted net profit for 2025. The company successfully completed the PWO integration, achieving EUR90 million in synergies, 34% more than initially announced. Lottomatica Group SpA (LOTMY) optimized its cost of debt with a successful refinancing, resulting in EUR24 million annual savings in interest costs. The company has entered the FTSE MIB and now has liquidity above EUR50 million per day, significantly higher than the IPO level. Lottomatica Group SpA (LOTMY) plans to return capital to shareholders through dividends and a buyback program, with a proposed dividend of EUR0.44 per share and authorization for an additional 12.5% share buyback.

Negative Points

The net leverage remains at 2.4x, which would have been lower without the buyback, indicating a potential constraint on financial flexibility. The gaming franchise segment showed minimal growth, with revenues up only 1% and EBITDA up 3%, indicating potential stagnation in this area. The upfront payment for the online concession CapEx increased significantly, impacting cash flow. The company faces ongoing pressure from smaller operators losing market share, which could impact competitive dynamics. The technical certification of new systems may require a slowdown in new product launches, potentially affecting growth momentum.

Story Continues

Q & A Highlights

Q: Can you explain the share capture dynamics, particularly excluding PWO's contribution? A: Guglielmo Angelozzi, CEO: The growth in market share is roughly half organic and half from acquisitions like Sportbet. The contribution from PWO was negative, so the legacy brands increased by 1.8 points. We believe this trend is sustainable due to a constructive competitive environment.

Q: How does the decision to allocate more capital towards buybacks relate to M&A opportunities? A: Laurence Van Lancker, CFO: The increase in buybacks reflects our accelerating cash flow generation and deleveraging. We continue to follow the same capital allocation framework, with excess cash going to buybacks, competing with M&A opportunities.

Q: Are there any updates on the retail concession and government discussions? A: Guglielmo Angelozzi, CEO: The government is refining the decree for the retail concession, and discussions with regions are ongoing. The setup remains constructive, but the process is still in the government's hands.

Q: How does the World Cup factor into your guidance, especially if Italy qualifies? A: Laurence Van Lancker, CFO: The World Cup is an opportunity for customer acquisition, but its impact on our guidance is minimal, whether Italy qualifies or not.

Q: Can you elaborate on the drivers behind the implied EBITDA margin guide, particularly in online? A: Laurence Van Lancker, CFO: The margin expansion is driven by the faster growth of online segments, which have higher margins, and the realization of synergies from the PWO integration.

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript.
